name: Default Conservative Tradecraft Rules
description: Evidence-based hypotheses for safely analyzed scripts and command lines. Findings do not independently establish malicious intent.

rules.title: Encoded or compressed content observed
rules.description: One or more non-input transformations were reconstructed. Encoding or compression can be legitimate, so this finding should be treated as an analytic hypothesis.
rules.confidence_basis: The transformation was directly reconstructed, but encoding and compression are broad behaviors with many legitimate uses.

rules.title: PowerShell interpreter observed
rules.description: The analyzed content referenced PowerShell or pwsh. This may represent normal administration or command and scripting interpreter activity.
rules.confidence_basis: An exact normalized PowerShell or pwsh command indicator was directly observed in an analyzed stage.

rules.title: Windows command shell observed
rules.description: The analyzed content referenced cmd or cmd.exe. This may represent normal administration or Windows command-shell activity.
rules.confidence_basis: An exact normalized cmd or cmd.exe command indicator was directly observed in an analyzed stage.
rules.attack_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell

rules.title: Mshta utility observed
rules.description: The analyzed content referenced mshta. Its presence supports a possible system-binary proxy-execution hypothesis but does not establish malicious use.
rules.confidence_basis: An exact mshta command indicator was directly observed, but mshta is a dual-use system utility and intent cannot be determined statically.

rules.title: Regsvr32 utility observed
rules.description: The analyzed content referenced regsvr32. Its presence supports a possible system-binary proxy-execution hypothesis but does not establish malicious use.
rules.confidence_basis: An exact regsvr32 command indicator was directly observed, but regsvr32 is a dual-use system utility and intent cannot be determined statically.

rules.title: Rundll32 utility observed
rules.description: The analyzed content referenced rundll32. Its presence supports a possible system-binary proxy-execution hypothesis but does not establish malicious use.
rules.confidence_basis: An exact rundll32 command indicator was directly observed, but rundll32 is a dual-use system utility and intent cannot be determined statically.
